hook_type: Cadence-of-three + 'none from VCs' curiosity punch, into a third-party validation excerpt
tactic: Numbers + no-gatekeeper punch -> Aubrey Blanche category quote -> the platform is raising -> waitlist
primary_text:
324 raises, $234M+, none from VCs.

"Crowdfunding opens up the ability to invest in excellent businesses to a significantly broader set of investors." Aubrey Blanche.

That is what 137,000 Australians did on our platform, owning a piece of companies like Naked Life and Our Cow. Now we are opening our own Crowd-Sourced Funding raise.

The waitlist is free. You read the offer document before you decide.

Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: 324 raises, $234M+, none from VCs
description: Investing that used to be VC-only. Save your spot.
_iterates: BIRCHAL-V4-A3 access DNA + new QUOTE-006 (Aubrey Blanche) third-party category validation.
_v5_rationale: Uses QUOTE-006 ad_safe excerpt verbatim, attributed 'Aubrey Blanche' (no invented title). Brief: this is third-party validation of the CSF CATEGORY, not a Birchal claim, so it sits as an external voice before the Birchal-specific proof, never merged with a Birchal stat.
Hook '324 raises, $234M+, none from VCs.' Halbert 5/5 in --primary-text mode (cadence + no-gatekeeper curiosity punch). Carried from the validated V3/V4 hook family.
'invest in excellent businesses' is inside Blanche's attributed quote (her word, exempt); the surrounding ad prose uses no transaction verb as a CTA. The reader's action remains 'save your spot'.

V5b: stronger email-capture CTA (FarmThru-proven pattern). Closing CTA changed from the weaker 'save your spot' framing to an explicit email-capture micro-action ('Leave your email...'), per clients/farm-thru/learnings-meta-ad.md verbatim ('Outcome-stated CTA: leave your email, we will tell you the moment it goes live'). Notification-framed, no transaction verb, AU English, no contractions, CSF-compliant. Risk-reversal sentence, hook, headline, proof cadence, fact citations, and the verbatim CSF warning all unchanged. Button cta field 'Save my spot' (approved WAITLIST-CTA verbatim) unchanged.
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors who feel startup investing is gated to VCs and family offices.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'we will tell you the moment', 'read'. No transaction verbs as a CTA. 'invest in excellent businesses' is inside Blanche's attributed verbatim, not a reader instruction.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
BRAND-EXCLUDED: only verified-good raises (Naked Life RAISE-001, Our Cow RAISE-002).

QUOTE-006: Aubrey Blanche ad_safe excerpt verbatim (contiguous sub-span), attributed 'Aubrey Blanche', no invented title. Used as CSF-category validation, NOT a Birchal-specific claim; not merged with any Birchal stat.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' appears zero times in body; 'our platform' / first-person we/our; one referent.
Number traceability: 324=PLAT-001, $234M+=PLAT-002, 137,000=PLAT-003, brands=RAISE-001/002. No market-share / #1 / first claim.
